reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about concurrent access to the s2s_cp_stateids IDR and the cp_list in nfs4_alloc_init_cpntf_state(). They agreed that the cs_type assignment and list_add() should be folded into the same critical section as idr_alloc_cyclic(), and added INIT_LIST_HEAD() to prevent stale unlink issues. The patch will need further revision.
sentiment_signals: concern about IDR and cp_list access

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the teardown lifetime of async copy objects, explaining that concurrent access to the same object could lead to double-putting and freeing of nfsd_file structures. The author proposed restructuring the code to ensure that each teardown caller removes the copy from clp->async_copies first, and then kthread_stop() runs exactly once. A fix is planned for v2.

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the s2s_cp_stateids IDR being pointed at reused request memory, acknowledged that this was a bug and agreed to duplicate the fields first and then register the stateid on the stable async_copy.
reviews.raw_body:
For an async COPY, nfsd4_copy() called nfs4_init_copy_state() before
dup_copy_fields(), so the s2s_cp_stateids IDR was pointed at
&u->copy->cp_stateid -- memory in the per-rqstp COMPOUND buffer that is
reused by the next request. dup_copy_fields() copies only the value into
async_copy, so the IDR slot dangled at the transient buffer for the whole
background copy. Any IDR walker then dereferences reused request memory:
the laundromat reads cs_type from it and, if the bytes look like an
expired NFS4_COPYNOTIFY_STID, follows into
refcount_dec()/idr_remove()/kfree() on garbage; manage_cpntf_state() has
the same exposure via idr_find().

Duplicate the fields first, then register the stateid on the stable
async_copy. result->cb_stateid is unchanged.

Fixes: e0639dc5805a ("NFSD introduce async copy feature")
Assisted-by: Claude:claude-opus-4-8
Signed-off-by: Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>
---
 fs/nfsd/nfs4proc.c | 7 ++++---
 1 file changed, 4 insertions(+), 3 deletions(-)

diff --git a/fs/nfsd/nfs4proc.c b/fs/nfsd/nfs4proc.c
index b2f1a9bc9202..b9049a3d7c07 100644
--- a/fs/nfsd/nfs4proc.c
+++ b/fs/nfsd/nfs4proc.c
@@ -2237,11 +2237,12 @@ nfsd4_copy(struct svc_rqst *rqstp, struct nfsd4_compound_state *cstate,
 		async_copy->cp_src = kmalloc_obj(*async_copy->cp_src);
 		if (!async_copy->cp_src)
 			goto out_dec_async_copy_err;
-		if (!nfs4_init_copy_state(nn, copy))
+		dup_copy_fields(copy, async_copy);
+
+		if (!nfs4_init_copy_state(nn, async_copy))
 			goto out_dec_async_copy_err;
-		memcpy(&result->cb_stateid, &copy->cp_stateid.cs_stid,
+		memcpy(&result->cb_stateid, &async_copy->cp_stateid.cs_stid,
 			sizeof(result->cb_stateid));
-		dup_copy_fields(copy, async_copy);
 		if ((READ_ONCE(copy->nf_dst->nf_file->f_mode) &
 			       FMODE_NOCMTIME) != 0)
 			async_copy->attr_update = true;

-- 
2.55.0

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about use-after-free in the nfsd4_copy_notify() function, where a racing OFFLOAD_CANCEL could free the cpntf stateid before it was fully initialized and published. The author agreed to restructure the code to record cp_p_stateid and cp_p_clid inside nfs4_alloc_init_cpntf_state() before publication, and return them with an extra reference.

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the OFFLOAD_CANCEL path in nfsd, where any authenticated NFSv4.2 client could cancel and free another client's copy-notify stateid due to lack of ownership check. The author agreed to fix this by comparing the creating clientid with the requesting client's cl_clientid and return nfserr_bad_stateid on mismatch.
reviews.raw_body:
On the OFFLOAD_CANCEL path (clp != NULL), manage_cpntf_state() freed the
target cpntf state without checking ownership. The lookup key
st->si_opaque.so_id is allocated cyclically (guessable) and the embedded
clientid is the fixed per-net nn->s2s_cp_cl_id, so any authenticated
NFSv4.2 client could cancel and free another client's copy-notify
stateid.

Compare the creating clientid recorded in state->cp_p_clid against the
requesting client's cl_clientid and return nfserr_bad_stateid on a
mismatch instead of freeing the entry.

Fixes: ce0887ac96d3 ("NFSD add nfs4 inter ssc to nfsd4_copy")
Assisted-by: Claude:claude-opus-4-8
Signed-off-by: Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>
---
 fs/nfsd/nfs4state.c | 14 ++++++++++++--
 1 file changed, 12 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

diff --git a/fs/nfsd/nfs4state.c b/fs/nfsd/nfs4state.c
index 9ae0f18121ef..b3fe163a148d 100644
--- a/fs/nfsd/nfs4state.c
+++ b/fs/nfsd/nfs4state.c
@@ -7905,10 +7905,20 @@ __be32 manage_cpntf_state(struct nfsd_net *nn, stateid_t *st,
 			state = NULL;
 			goto unlock;
 		}
-		if (!clp)
+		if (!clp) {
 			refcount_inc(&state->cp_stateid.cs_count);
-		else
+		} else if (memcmp(&clp->cl_clientid, &state->cp_p_clid,
+				  sizeof(clientid_t))) {
+			/*
+			 * OFFLOAD_CANCEL: only the creating client may cancel.
+			 * so_id is guessable, so without this check any client
+			 * could free another's cpntf state.
+			 */
+			state = NULL;
+			goto unlock;
+		} else {
 			_free_cpntf_state_locked(nn, state);
+		}
 	}
 unlock:
 	spin_unlock(&nn->s2s_cp_lock);

-- 
2.55.0

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about concurrent revoke paths causing use-after-free bugs in the copy-notify stateid IDR and sc_cp_list. They added a new function, revoke_cpntf_state_locked(), which unhashes an entry from the IDR and sc_cp_list before dropping its reference, to prevent this issue. This fix affects all three revoke paths: parent-stid drain, OFFLOAD_CANCEL, and laundromat expiry.

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the handling of unsupported netloc4 types in nfsd4_decode_nl4_server(). They agreed that NL4_NAME and NL4_URL are well-formed XDR but not supported, and explained that they will decode and discard the utf8str_cis to keep the stream consistent, returning NFS4ERR_NOTSUPP instead of NFS4ERR_BADXDR. A fix is planned for v2.

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the dual identity of struct nfsd4_copy causing lifetime bugs by introducing a new durable object, struct nfsd4_async_copy, which embeds a struct nfsd4_copy for operation parameters/result and adds durable-only fields. The author explained that this is a mechanical split with no intended behavioral change, a step toward folding copy stateids into the common nfs4_stid infrastructure.

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the async COPY offload stateid being shared with COPY_NOTIFY stateids in the same IDR, acknowledged that this was problematic and agreed to restructure it by promoting the async copy object to a first-class nfs4_stid allocated from the client's cl_stateids. The lifetime model for the async copy remains unchanged.

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary: Reviewer Chuck Lever applied the patch series to nfsd-testing and thanked Jeff Layton for the patches, indicating a positive sentiment.
reviews.raw_body:
Applied to nfsd-testing, thanks!

[01/10] nfsd: fix cpntf publish race in nfs4_init_cp_state
        commit: 2644ead835d12198cceef3c407c25ed94717b378
[02/10] nfsd: fix UAF in async copy cancel and shutdown
        commit: a3cddbf9713ae5ac03915b29975dc200ea6493fe
[03/10] nfsd: fix stale s2s_cp_stateids IDR entry for async COPY
        commit: 527c422ddaa3e0a452658faca10d1634f151ca6b
[04/10] nfsd: initialize copy-notify stateid before publishing it
        commit: 23fc79d2a8869ab25f0de5642a0f10d030d67d05
[05/10] nfsd: check client ownership when cancelling a copy-notify stateid
        commit: ee6c8255190685955762f66d23a887c4f86d24c6
[06/10] nfsd: revoke copy-notify stateids before dropping their reference
        commit: 24dd7409daafc00a0f19b61cb91d8f051cdc6b4b
[07/10] nfsd: return NFS4ERR_NOTSUPP for unsupported netloc4 types
        commit: 673c92979f3ab32c92df687625eee76ce42e55ed
[08/10] nfsd: split nfsd4_copy into transient and durable async copy objects
        commit: bf9b4f03a032cc111a6ec707627964f23c9b1209
[09/10] nfsd: make the copy offload stateid a first-class nfs4_stid
        commit: 74d681696ac194f355a2fc21693a44c816715bd2
[10/10] nfsd: drop dead COPY-vs-COPYNOTIFY type handling from s2s stateid IDR
        commit: 9b33df55dcac6950d1e176c7bb02a127d2d3dc1f

--
Chuck Lever

2026-07-10.patch_summary: This patch series addresses bugs in the inter-server copy offload code for NFSd, specifically issues related to stateid management and object lifetimes. The patches fix problems found through kres inspection and agentic review, and rework the code to be less prone to future issues. The changes include splitting the nfsd4_copy object into two separate objects with well-defined lifetimes, integrating COPY stateids into the normal nfs4_stid model, and fixing potential bugs such as IDR leaks and UAFs. Additionally, the patches improve comments and add new tests for COPY functionality.
